mechanism_narrative: Positive PAYEMS momentum signals improving household income and lower recession risk, so months with rising payroll employment predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if PAYEMS growth has nonpositive Rank IC with 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of rolling 3-year walk-forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate crossing below 0.25% identifies an effective zero-rate regime that lowers discount rates and raises equity risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses below 0.25% versus its prior monthly observation
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary easing regime
failure_condition: Reject if triggered zero-rate observations deliver average 60-day S&P 500 returns below the unconditional average or a hit rate below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising RSAFS indicates stronger consumer spending and near-term revenue growth for listed firms, so positive retail sales momentum predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS momentum is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in less than 50% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in INDPRO signals weakening factory output and falling operating leverage, so industrial production contraction predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO contraction triggers are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward cases.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE is above 7% and FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, falling unemployment signals post-shock labor healing while policy remains easy, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE latest snapshot value is 7.6 and FEDFUNDS latest snapshot value is 0.11
failure_condition: Reject if the joint high-unemployment low-rate regime does not improve the 20-day return hit rate versus using UNRATE alone by at least 5 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Rising HOUST under low FEDFUNDS signals a rate-sensitive housing recovery that lifts construction activity and cyclicals, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 1.0% and HOUST 3-month percent change crosses above 0%
failure_condition: Reject if the HOUST and FEDFUNDS interaction has lower 60-day predictive Rank IC than HOUST momentum alone over two consecutive 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating CPIAUCSL while FEDFUNDS is pinned near zero raises expected policy normalization and margin pressure, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month percent change crosses above its 12-month percent change while FEDFUNDS < 0.25%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered CPI acceleration episodes have nonnegative average 5-day S&P 500 returns or fail to underperform the unconditional 5-day average.

mechanism_narrative: Positive GDPC1 growth signals broad real demand expansion and stronger aggregate earnings capacity, so rising real GDP predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive GDPC1 growth is associated with below-median 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of walk-forward observations.
